name: Shorbat Lsan El AsfoorMEATBALL & ORZO SOUP
intro: Whenever I make kofta or meatballs, I always make sure to keep some in the fridge to make this soup the next day. Orzo is called lsan el asfour in Arabic, which translates to “bird’s tongue,” after the pasta shape. I remember how fun I used to think it was to pretend to be eating birds’ tongues when I was little. I make the meatballs for this soup extra small, because somehow they are more satisfying to eat that way in one bite, along with the orzo. This is my kids’ favorite soup.

ingredients.item: kosher salt, plus more if needed

method: In a medium bowl, combine all the meatball ingredients beef, salt, pepper, seven spice, onion and garlic powders, paprika, and bread crumbs and mix well using your hands. Roll into ½-inch (13 mm) meatballs.
In a medium pot, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the meatballs, working in batches, and sear for 2 to 3 minutes, until golden on most sides. Transfer to a plate.

In the same pot, add the oil, onion, and salt and cook, stirring often, for 5 to 6 minutes, until softened and translucent. Add the garlic and cook, stirring often, for a few minutes, or until fragrant. Add the tomato paste and stir for a few seconds.

Pour in the broth and add the meatballs back into the pot. Cover the pot with the lid and bring to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium and let simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.

Remove the lid and add the orzo. Stir well, then cover and let cook for 15 minutes. Taste and adjust the soup for salt if necessary. Check the orzo to ensure it is cooked to al dente, or softer if desired.
Ladle into bowls, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve with pita bread.
